title: Consensus-backed metadata rollup and reader-friendly changelog
preview: Context and motivation: We onboard models from a variety of sources (author-uploaded manifests, CI-provided metadata, and discovery probes). Discrepancies between sources (missing fields, differing perf metrics, variant aliases) have caused noisy changelogs and occasional release regressions. This PR implements a de...
text:
Context and motivation: We onboard models from a variety of sources (author-uploaded manifests, CI-provided metadata, and discovery probes). Discrepancies between sources (missing fields, differing perf metrics, variant aliases) have caused noisy changelogs and occasional release regressions. This PR implements a deterministic "consensus rollup" step during intake that: 1) canonicalizes manifest keys and field formats; 2) merges performance profile vectors using a configurable aggregator (weighted median by signal confidence); 3) records provenance for each field so downstream systems can explain where a value came from; and 4) emits a concise, reader-friendly changelog "teaser" that groups changes into: metadata edits, perf deltas, and variant-level lifecycle events.

What changed (high level):
- New consensus rollup service (registry/rollup) that takes a set of manifests and returns...

title: Contextual release pruner and variant signal hash
preview: Problem: onboarding pages and release notes for model variants accumulate many low-signal items that hide deployment risks. This PR implements a contextual release pruner that selects a small, high-signal subset of changelog fragments and pairs it with a compact variant signal hash — a deterministic, size-bounded di...
text: Problem: onboarding pages and release notes for model variants accumulate many low-signal items that hide deployment risks. This PR implements a contextual release pruner that selects a small, high-signal subset of changelog fragments and pairs it with a compact variant signal hash — a deterministic, size-bounded digest derived from sampled telemetry features — to help reviewers and downstream tools quickly assess runtime affinity. Key ideas: (1) Variant signal hash condenses anonymized telemetry-derived features (sequence length distribution quantiles, common token-prefixes, average KV cache hit hint) into a stable 64-char hex digest. The hash is produced from deterministic sketching of reservoir-sampled telemetry aggregates so it is reproducible across uploads and small enough for manifests. (2) Release pruner ranks candidate changelog fragments by a hybrid score (telemetry-weighted...

title: Introduce compact stability ledger and change-contextualizer for snapshot diffs
preview: Motivation and summary: Large snapshot onboarding produced verbose profile blobs and brittle release notes; this PR provides a compact, append-only 'stability ledger' designed to store sparse stability signals and provenance per snapshot plus a change contextualizer that synthesizes concise release briefs from ledge...
text: Motivation and summary: Large snapshot onboarding produced verbose profile blobs and brittle release notes; this PR provides a compact, append-only 'stability ledger' designed to store sparse stability signals and provenance per snapshot plus a change contextualizer that synthesizes concise release briefs from ledger deltas and sampled eval signatures. Implementation highlights: ledger implementation in registry/ledger/stability.py introduces a tight binary+JSON entry format keyed by snapshot hash ribbon (sha256_base58). Each entry keeps stability_token (low/medium/high), a small latency_histogram_bucketed summary, token_error_rate as a fixed-point string, and a truncated provenance string; typical entry size is <1KB. The diff serializer at registry/snapshots/diff_serializer.py emits compact delta blobs containing tensor fingerprints, model config deltas, and profile delta summaries (...

title: Introduce mini-prober and cost-signature autodoc for lightweight model intake
preview: Context and motivation: Model onboarding at scale needs a lightweight way to capture representative cost signals (average sequence length, token distribution, cache-friendliness) without running full telemetry. This PR adds a small, safe prober that runs a bounded sampling workload during onboarding and produces a c...
text:
Context and motivation: Model onboarding at scale needs a lightweight way to capture representative cost signals (average sequence length, token distribution, cache-friendliness) without running full telemetry. This PR adds a small, safe prober that runs a bounded sampling workload during onboarding and produces a compact 'cost-signature' artifact. The platform uses the signature to offer conservative cost guidance and to insert an autodrafted changelog entry when a model is onboarded or updated.

What I changed (high level): Added a mini-prober library and CLI wiring, a cost-signature serializer embedded in model metadata, a release-drafter hook that uses the signature to create a simple cost blurb, and tests/fixtures. The prober is intentionally conservative: max 50 sample prompts, short sequence caps, no external dataset downloads. It runs only when --mini-probe is enabled or when ...

title: introduce-release-lighthouse-and-versioned-metrics-extractor
preview: Motivation: release notes generation has become a bottleneck during model onboarding. Teams repeatedly rewrite similar perf/context blurb templates and manually reconcile metric snapshots for each model variant. This PR introduces a "release lighthouse" extractor that synthesizes a candidate human-friendly release s...
text:
Motivation: release notes generation has become a bottleneck during model onboarding. Teams repeatedly rewrite similar perf/context blurb templates and manually reconcile metric snapshots for each model variant. This PR introduces a "release lighthouse" extractor that synthesizes a candidate human-friendly release synopsis and a versioned-metrics artifact (time-stamped, normalized vectors) for each onboarding run so downstream docs and the Console can consume consistent, auditable release snippets.

Design summary: the feature adds a pipeline step (registry.ingest -> lighthouse.extract -> artifact.store) that: (1) samples keyed perf snapshots (latency P50/P95, tokens/sec, mem), (2) runs lightweight heuristics to surface regressions and noteworthy regressions, (3) generates a short prose synopsis with labeled highlights (throughput, footprint, notable regressions) and optional mitigati...

title: Introduce onboard lineage map and autorelease anchor synthesizer
preview: Problem: When onboarding models we lacked a machine-readable lineage artifact that connects model package uploads, profiling runs, and generated release notes. That made automated changelog synthesis brittle and required manual stitching for multi-variant releases. This PR adds two pieces: (1) a compact onboard line...
text:
Problem: When onboarding models we lacked a machine-readable lineage artifact that connects model package uploads, profiling runs, and generated release notes. That made automated changelog synthesis brittle and required manual stitching for multi-variant releases. This PR adds two pieces: (1) a compact onboard lineage map that records upstream artifacts (source repo, dataset snapshot id, build hash, profiling snapshot id) and (2) an autorelease anchor synthesizer that produces a canonical release anchor and draft changelog entries based on lineage and profile diffs.

Design summary: The onboard lineage map is emitted as part of the onboarding pipeline and stored in the registry as a lightweight provenance document. It uses stable identifiers (dataset-snap, build-hash, profile-seed) and explicit relation types (built-from, profiled-by, variant-of). The autorelease anchor synthesizer c...

title: add provenance-backed metadata enricher and performance-stamp publisher
preview: Motivation: When models are onboarded we lack a compact, auditable performance signature attached to the model metadata that can be consumed by release note automation and ops tooling. This PR introduces a provenance-backed metadata enricher that synthesizes canonical metadata fields (fingerprint, hardware-affinity ...
text: Motivation: When models are onboarded we lack a compact, auditable performance signature attached to the model metadata that can be consumed by release note automation and ops tooling. This PR introduces a provenance-backed metadata enricher that synthesizes canonical metadata fields (fingerprint, hardware-affinity hints, sample-derived latency/p90/cost stamps) and a separate performance-stamp publisher that emits a small, versioned artifact used by the changelog/autodraft hook to populate 'performance notes' in release drafts. Scope: 1) onboarding enricher service (onboard/enricher) that runs post-ingest to compute and attach a performance signature and provenance ledger entry; 2) publisher service (publisher/stamp_publisher) that signs and stores a compact JSON stamp in model artifacts storage and emits an event for release automation; 3) small CLI hook (hooks/autodraft_publisher) c...

title: Rollup compat-map and onboarding metadata adapter with changelog seed backfill
preview: Motivation: We have overlapping compatibility entries across model variants that complicate release note generation and rollout heuristics. This PR introduces a compatibility rollup step that composes pairwise compat entries into a compact matrix and a lightweight metadata adapter that normalizes incoming onboard pa...
text:
Motivation: We have overlapping compatibility entries across model variants that complicate release note generation and rollout heuristics. This PR introduces a compatibility rollup step that composes pairwise compat entries into a compact matrix and a lightweight metadata adapter that normalizes incoming onboard payloads (provider, quantization tags, variant hints) so downstream profile generation and changelog seeds are stable.

What this PR does: - Adds a compat-rollup module that deduplicates and collapses pairwise compat rules into a hierarchical matrix keyed by model family/version. - Adds metadata-adapter layer which runs as a normalization pass during onboarding and when re-ingesting legacy manifests. - Adds a changelog-seed backfill migration that synthesizes short human-friendly blurbs for recent onboardings so release assembler has content to surface. - Adds unit + integrat...

title: curation-playbook-and-changelog-translation-hook
preview: Motivation: release notes and compact changelogs generated by the model registry are currently English-first and optimized for machine consumption. Customers and internal stakeholders have requested two things: 1) better human-readable, concise release blurbs that can be surfaced in the Console and email digests; 2)...
text:
Motivation: release notes and compact changelogs generated by the model registry are currently English-first and optimized for machine consumption. Customers and internal stakeholders have requested two things: 1) better human-readable, concise release blurbs that can be surfaced in the Console and email digests; 2) localized (i18n) variants of those blurbs so non-English engineering and product teams can review and approve model rollouts without translation bottlenecks. This PR introduces a lightweight curation playbook and a changelog translation/templating hook that sit in the onboard -> release-assembler pipeline.

What this PR does (high level):
- Adds a curation playbook module (src/curation/playbook.py) that codifies checks and heuristics used during model onboarding: perf delta thresholds, sample-based sanity checks, affinity/quantization compatibility hints, and human-review ...

title: Expose canonical model+version identifiers for runtime telemetry
preview: Context: perf-canary baselines and alerting are keyed per model. Today, runtime telemetry frequently emits an unstable identifier (human alias, internal deployment name, or a resolved artifact pointer) depending on routing and control-plane config. That causes baseline fragmentation (same model -> multiple time seri...
text:
Context: perf-canary baselines and alerting are keyed per model. Today, runtime telemetry frequently emits an unstable identifier (human alias, internal deployment name, or a resolved artifact pointer) depending on routing and control-plane config. That causes baseline fragmentation (same model -> multiple time series) and false regressions during rollouts or alias updates. This PR adds a canonical, registry-owned model identifier to the Model Registry "resolve" response and ensures the identifier is suitable for telemetry and long-lived aggregation.

What this changes: 1) Introduces canonical identifiers: model_id (stable UUID), model_slug (stable string, e.g. "llama-3-8b-instruct"), and model_version (registry semantic version or registry version id) as first-class fields in registry responses. 2) Extends the ResolveModel API to return canonical_model and canonical_version alongside...

title: introduce-cohort-sample-manifests-and-watermark-backed-change-drafter
preview: Motivation: onboarding teams asked for smaller, targeted sample sets for release notes and regressions that map to customer cohorts (large-enterprise, mobile-first, helpdesk). In addition we need a way to produce concise changelog drafts that preserve sample provenance but remove identifying tokens: a watermark-back...
text: Motivation: onboarding teams asked for smaller, targeted sample sets for release notes and regressions that map to customer cohorts (large-enterprise, mobile-first, helpdesk). In addition we need a way to produce concise changelog drafts that preserve sample provenance but remove identifying tokens: a watermark-backed drafting flow that ties a compact diff summary to a sampled manifest. Design: add a CohortSample manifest (schema v2) that declares sample cohorts, deterministic seed, and retention/privacy tags; implement a watermark drafters subsystem that can generate compact changelog teasers (one-line impact bullets + representative sample snippets) tied to a sample manifest hash and signature. The drafter uses K-anonymized snippet trimming and an optional token-redaction pipeline. Implementation: 1) manifest schema and validation (manifest/v2) + CLI preview (onboard-sample preview ...

title: Introduce shingled onboard metadata and size-aware release extract
preview: Background and motivation: onboarding very large model metadata bundles (profiles, test vectors, long-form provenance) has repeatedly caused large PR payloads and slow downstream release note generation. Large deltas were both noisy and expensive to store/serialize, and the release assembler often generated verbose ...
text:
Background and motivation: onboarding very large model metadata bundles (profiles, test vectors, long-form provenance) has repeatedly caused large PR payloads and slow downstream release note generation. Large deltas were both noisy and expensive to store/serialize, and the release assembler often generated verbose changelogs that made it hard for release owners to triage. This PR introduces a shingled metadata serializer for onboard payloads (metadata shingling) and a size-aware release extract pipeline that samples and compresses changelog candidates to produce concise release notes. Goals: reduce payload size during model onboarding, make changelog summaries readable by humans, and provide deterministic, auditable release extracts for downstream automation.

What I changed:
- onboard/serializer: add shingle_encoder.py (shingled metadata encoding). The encoder splits large metadata ...

title: Introduce workload-conditioned profile compositor and changelog preview generator
preview: Motivation: model onboarding currently produces multiple performance profiles from different probes (latency, token throughput, memory watermark) and from targeted synthetic workloads, but downstream consumers (serving, cost estimator, release notes) need a single coherent profile per variant. Manual merging is erro...
text:
Motivation: model onboarding currently produces multiple performance profiles from different probes (latency, token throughput, memory watermark) and from targeted synthetic workloads, but downstream consumers (serving, cost estimator, release notes) need a single coherent profile per variant. Manual merging is error-prone and hides important workload-dependent behavior. This PR adds a workload-conditioned profile compositor that deterministically merges multiple probe-derived profiles into a set of workload-conditioned variants (chat, summarization, long-context, embedding-heavy). It also introduces a changelog preview generator that synthesizes concise release notes describing the net change introduced by a new composite profile relative to the last released profile. The generated changelog is intended as a review artifact for curation and release ops.

What this change does: implem...

title: Add onboard-priority heuristics and contextual changelog extractor
preview: Context and motivation: As the intake stream grows, reviewers and curators are overwhelmed by raw onboard artifacts. This PR introduces a priority-scoring heuristic and an automated, context-aware changelog extractor to make onboarding reviews and release notes generation actionable and concise. The heuristic combin...
text: Context and motivation: As the intake stream grows, reviewers and curators are overwhelmed by raw onboard artifacts. This PR introduces a priority-scoring heuristic and an automated, context-aware changelog extractor to make onboarding reviews and release notes generation actionable and concise. The heuristic combines telemetry (sampled latency/throughput), footprint (size, quantization), legal signals (license, embargo), and delta impact (perf regressions vs baseline) into a single probabilistic priority score. The changelog extractor uses metadata diffs, curated prompt-set examples, and example-based summarization rules to produce one-line and paragraph-level release snippets suitable for downstream release automation. Key changes: 1) New pipeline: services/onboard/priority_scorer.py (probabilistic ranker, supports pluggable feature transforms) 2) Migration: db/migrations/20251103_a...

title: Add weighted probe sampler and autogen performance hint annotations
preview: Summary: Introduces a weighted probe-sampling utility and an optional 'performance_hints' metadata annotation for model profiles. The sampler produces small, representative workload slices used by the registry to auto-generate suggested batching/quantization profiles and to estimate cost/latency impact for release n...
text:
Summary: Introduces a weighted probe-sampling utility and an optional 'performance_hints' metadata annotation for model profiles. The sampler produces small, representative workload slices used by the registry to auto-generate suggested batching/quantization profiles and to estimate cost/latency impact for release notes.
Motivation: onboarding new models currently relies on heavy full-workload profiling or manual hinting. This change reduces friction by (1) providing a lightweight sampled footprint for quick calibrations, and (2) surfacing automated performance hints that can be included in changelogs and the model catalog.
Context and linked work: implements parts of ENG-1428 (deploy-time perf hint generation) and ties back to SUP-7241 (customer report: slow cold-starts for large decoder-only models).
Design notes:
- Weighted sampling: we sample examples from a model's telemetry or s...

title: Version claim notarizer and micro-benchmark replay harness
preview: Problem: Onboarding teams were seeing transient mismatches between declared model version metadata and the performance stamps we publish. That led to confusion when a variant rolled with differing microbenchmark characteristics, which in turn caused rollout regressions. This PR introduces two complementary subsystem...
text:
Problem: Onboarding teams were seeing transient mismatches between declared model version metadata and the performance stamps we publish. That led to confusion when a variant rolled with differing microbenchmark characteristics, which in turn caused rollout regressions. This PR introduces two complementary subsystems: a version-claim notarizer that enforces deterministic, signed version claims for model uploads and a micro-benchmark replay harness that deterministically replays a curated micro-benchmark suite to generate reproducible performance stamps.

Design summary: 1) Version claim notarizer: a lightweight preflight hook that validates incoming model metadata, computes a canonical claim (content hash + schema-normalized metadata), attaches a notarization token, and writes a provenance entry to the registry ledger. The notarizer supports optional KMS signing for enterprise Private...

title: Introduce declarative release annotations and runtime profile hinting
preview:
Context and motivation:
We frequently onboard new models with performance guidance buried in PR bodies or internal wiki pages. That makes it hard for the serving runtime and release automation to pick optimal defaults (quantization, batch sizing, routing policy) and for release notes tools to produce consistent chan...
text:
Context and motivation:
We frequently onboard new models with performance guidance buried in PR bodies or internal wiki pages. That makes it hard for the serving runtime and release automation to pick optimal defaults (quantization, batch sizing, routing policy) and for release notes tools to produce consistent changelogs. This PR introduces a small, intentionally-declarative annotation layer on model release manifests and ties those annotations into the changelog generator and onboarding checks.

What this PR does (high-level):
1) Adds a new optional top-level 'release_annotations' block to model manifest schema. The block is expressive but constrained: keys include "recommended_quant", "latency_ms_p99", "preferred_batching" (list), "compat_notes", and "release_reason".
2) Implements a parser and validator (registry/annotations/validator.py) that enforces allowed keys, ranges (e.g., ...

title: Migrate model records to metadata v1 (backfill + rollback plan)
preview: Context: As part of the model metadata schema v1 rollout (see pr-18421), we need to migrate existing model/variant records so the registry can serve metadata_v1 consistently and unblock changelog generation + Console change history. Today, key fields are split across legacy columns and ad-hoc JSON; some records are ...
text: Context: As part of the model metadata schema v1 rollout (see pr-18421), we need to migrate existing model/variant records so the registry can serve metadata_v1 consistently and unblock changelog generation + Console change history. Today, key fields are split across legacy columns and ad-hoc JSON; some records are incomplete and some are deployment-specific. This PR introduces a forward-only schema migration, a backfill job, and an explicit rollback procedure that preserves read-compatibility during the rollout window. Summary of changes: 1) DB schema: add metadata_v1 columns (jsonb) for model_versions and model_variants, plus schema_hash and migration bookkeeping (backfilled_at, backfill_source, backfill_warnings). Add partial indexes for common queries (model_id + version, availability filters). 2) Backfill job: new admin command `registry-admin backfill-metadata-v1` that transform...

title: introduce-model-accession-canonicalization-and-release-tape
preview: Motivation: onboarding produced multiple informal identifiers for the same model (aliases, git tags, local upload ids). That ambiguity propagates into changelogs, rollback tooling, and audit exports. This PR introduces a compact canonical 'Model Accession' (MA) assigned at onboarding and a small, signed 'release tap...
text:
Motivation: onboarding produced multiple informal identifiers for the same model (aliases, git tags, local upload ids). That ambiguity propagates into changelogs, rollback tooling, and audit exports. This PR introduces a compact canonical 'Model Accession' (MA) assigned at onboarding and a small, signed 'release tape' artifact that bundles the accession, profile snapshot, and a provenance hash for release-note automation.

What this PR does: 1) Adds an accession generator and persistence layer: a short, globally-unique accession (MA-YYYY-<base32>) recorded on model create. 2) Adds a release-tape assembler (lightweight JSONL artifact) that contains the accession, model metadata canonicalized, performance-profile snapshot, and a provenance hash signed by the registry key. 3) Adds 'profile guards' to validate incoming performance profiles against accountable thresholds (latency P95, mem ...

title: Introduce ephemeral onboarding probe and annotated change tester
preview: Motivation: onboarding new models currently records static profiles and full-sample performance snapshots which are heavy and slow. Teams often need a lightweight, low-cost sanity pass that validates metadata, short sequence performance, and quick compatibility checks without generating full canonical profiles. This...
text:
Motivation: onboarding new models currently records static profiles and full-sample performance snapshots which are heavy and slow. Teams often need a lightweight, low-cost sanity pass that validates metadata, short sequence performance, and quick compatibility checks without generating full canonical profiles. This PR adds two complementary pieces: (1) an ephemeral probe runner (epprobe) that executes short synthetic and sampled real workloads against a candidate model to produce a concise validation artifact, and (2) an annotated change tester that computes a delta against the previous release snapshot and drafts suggested changelog impact lines (latency, memory, tokenizer incompatibilities) for reviewers.

What this change does: epprobe is a small CLI + service hook that runs a configurable set of micro-benchmarks (short sequences, prefix cache warmups, tokenization sanity) and emi...

title: Introduce cohorted intake sampler and annotated release bundler
preview: Motivation: onboarding churn for large model uploads produced long, noisy changelogs that were hard for reviewers to triage. This PR implements a cohort-aware intake sampler that selects representative probes per model variant and a release bundler that packages compact annotated changelog snippets plus provenance a...
text: Motivation: onboarding churn for large model uploads produced long, noisy changelogs that were hard for reviewers to triage. This PR implements a cohort-aware intake sampler that selects representative probes per model variant and a release bundler that packages compact annotated changelog snippets plus provenance and cohort-coverage metrics. Implementation: 1) ingest/cohort_sampler: deterministic cohort selection seeded by model manifest fingerprint; supports weighted cohorts, stratified sampling across datasets and input lengths, and optional sampling profiles passed from the CLI or CI. 2) bundler/release_bundler: consumes sampler output, computes a canonical provenance hash for the intake bundle, synthesizes concise changelog fragments prioritized by regression score and coverage delta, and emits a compact release bundle artifact (ndjson + metadata.json). 3) CLI: onboardctl additio...

title: introduce-cohorted-affinity-change-summarizer-and-evolution-tracker
preview: Motivation: As teams onboard more model variants and tune performance profiles, release notes become noisy and obscure the true user-facing impact. This PR adds a cohort-aware affinity summarizer and evolution tracker which groups model metadata deltas by production cohort affinity and synthesizes concise changelog ...
text:
Motivation: As teams onboard more model variants and tune performance profiles, release notes become noisy and obscure the true user-facing impact. This PR adds a cohort-aware affinity summarizer and evolution tracker which groups model metadata deltas by production cohort affinity and synthesizes concise changelog snippets for release notes and the onboarding UI.

What it does (single continuous description): Adds a background worker that computes cohort affinity vectors for models (based on observed telemetry and declared affordances), records per-profile delta hashes, and generates human-readable changelog fragments that prioritize changes likely to affect high-affinity cohorts. The change includes: DB schema migration (model_profiles.affinity_vector, changelog_fragments table), API endpoints (/v1/registries/{model}/changelog-preview), a CLI subcommand (mr changelog preview --cohor...

title: Synthetic probe playback and release-slate generator
preview: Motivation: We need a deterministic way to replay synthetic probe results and synthesize a candidate "release slate" (compact set of suggested release notes and risk tags) for new model variants so reviewers can triage onboarding impact without running full ingestion. This PR introduces a playback engine that replay...
text: Motivation: We need a deterministic way to replay synthetic probe results and synthesize a candidate "release slate" (compact set of suggested release notes and risk tags) for new model variants so reviewers can triage onboarding impact without running full ingestion. This PR introduces a playback engine that replays previously captured synthetic-probe traces at configurable sample densities and a release-slate generator that produces a human-readable brief with risk indicators, sample performance deltas, and suggested changelog bullets. Design: 1) probe-playback service: accepts a probe trace id and density param, replays tokens/latency/error streams against candidate model variants in a sandbox environment, and emits condensed metrics. 2) release-slate generator: takes condensed metrics + profile metadata and produces a prioritized list of release bullets, per-route latency histogra...

title: Add benchmark serializer and release timeliner
preview: Why: onboarding produces many per-variant benchmark artifacts (latency histograms, P50/P95 traces, throughput stamps). We need a compact, versioned on-disk format so the registry can store, index, and compose those artifacts into human-facing release notes and machine-checkable regression timelines. This PR introduc...
text:
Why: onboarding produces many per-variant benchmark artifacts (latency histograms, P50/P95 traces, throughput stamps). We need a compact, versioned on-disk format so the registry can store, index, and compose those artifacts into human-facing release notes and machine-checkable regression timelines. This PR introduces a small, portable 'benchmark serializer' and a 'release timeliner' service that composes micro-release entries into a chronological digest for changelogs and downgrade/rollout playbooks.

What: single PR that implements: (1) bench serializer (binary+JSON compact representation with v1 header and stable field ordering); (2) timeliner CLI and library that consumes serialized benchmark artifacts and emits a microrelease timeline (JSONL) and a condensed markdown digest for release notes; (3) CI hook to assemble timeline artifacts and attach a release digest draft to the line...

title: add-transitive-dependency-graph-and-rolling-profile-calibration-hook
preview: Motivation: onboarding new model families has become harder to maintain when a single upstream weight change ripples through variants (quantized builds, fused kernels, etc). We need a canonical, machine-friendly representation of transitive model dependencies and an automated calibration job that re-evaluates perfor...
text:
Motivation: onboarding new model families has become harder to maintain when a single upstream weight change ripples through variants (quantized builds, fused kernels, etc). We need a canonical, machine-friendly representation of transitive model dependencies and an automated calibration job that re-evaluates performance profiles on a small set of canonical prompts to keep profile metadata current and to generate compact delta release notes.

What this PR does: adds a lightweight transitive-dependency graph to model metadata, implements a rolling-profile-calibration hook that runs in a low-cost sandbox, and wires the calibration output into the changelog assembler so we can publish concise, delta-based release notes for profile updates. The calibration job is conservative by design: it flags changes, produces a delta bundle, and only updates a profile stamp when thresholds are met or ...

title: Add rw/qwen2.5-72b-instruct to hosted catalog (profiles + provenance)
preview:
Adds a new Hosted API catalog model entry: `rw/qwen2.5-72b-instruct` with default quant profile selection and explicit provenance hashes.

Context
- Requested via ENG-4219 (Solutions: enterprise customer wants Qwen2.5 family parity)
- Applied ML eval gates: all suites passed (links below)
- Runtime perf bar: passed ...
text:
Adds a new Hosted API catalog model entry: `rw/qwen2.5-72b-instruct` with default quant profile selection and explicit provenance hashes.

Context
- Requested via ENG-4219 (Solutions: enterprise customer wants Qwen2.5 family parity)
- Applied ML eval gates: all suites passed (links below)
- Runtime perf bar: passed on H100 and L40S with `int8-weight-only` as default
- Security risk review: approved-with-restrictions (see link)

Checklist
- [x] Model ID + alias naming follows standard
- [x] Tokenizer identifier recorded
- [x] Context length limits set (2k/8k/16k)
- [x] Quant profiles added: fp16, int8-weight-only, int4-weight-only (int4 marked N/A on L40S)
- [x] Fallback policy set (fallback to rw/qwen2.5-72b-instruct-fp16 for first 7 days)
- [x] Provenance: sha256 hashes recorded for weights + tokenizer
- [x] Docs note filed (DEVEX-1182)

title: Add v1 quantization profile schema, sample profiles, and registry-side validation
preview: This PR introduces the v1 Quantization Profile schema to the model registry, adds example profile JSONs for a small set of initial model variants, and wires validation into the registry publish/update path. The goal is to ensure profiles are versionable, auditable, and safe to consume by downstream control plane/run...
text:
This PR introduces the v1 Quantization Profile schema to the model registry, adds example profile JSONs for a small set of initial model variants, and wires validation into the registry publish/update path. The goal is to ensure profiles are versionable, auditable, and safe to consume by downstream control plane/runtime code by enforcing strict constraints at the point of publication.

Summary of changes: 1) Add `quantization_profile.schema.json` (v1) and a typed validator wrapper used by the profile ingestion codepath. 2) Add sample profiles under `examples/quantization_profiles/v1/` to serve as canonical references for Applied ML authors and for CI fixtures. 3) Enforce validation rules in `RegistryProfileService.publishQuantProfile()` and `RegistryProfileService.updateQuantProfile()` with clear error messages intended to surface in API responses and internal tooling. 4) Add unit tes...

title: Versioned quantization profile artifact storage + signing/integrity verification
preview: This PR adds versioned artifact storage for Quantization Profile Library profiles and enforces integrity checks (sha256 + signature verification) on fetch. Motivation: profiles are referenced from route config and must be auditable/reproducible; we need deterministic resolution to an immutable artifact, with a clear...
text:
This PR adds versioned artifact storage for Quantization Profile Library profiles and enforces integrity checks (sha256 + signature verification) on fetch. Motivation: profiles are referenced from route config and must be auditable/reproducible; we need deterministic resolution to an immutable artifact, with a clear deprecation/supersedence story and guardrails against tampering/corruption in object storage.

What’s included:
- Versioned profile artifacts: profile versions now resolve to an immutable `artifact_ref` that points to a content-addressed blob plus a stable registry path.
- Manifest + checksums: each artifact upload produces a manifest with sha256 digests for each payload file and the overall bundle.
- Signing: registry signs the manifest (ed25519) using a KMS-backed key; clients verify signatures before accepting the artifact.
- Integrity verification on read path: registr...

title: add runtime-affinity prober and changelog-hint exporter
preview:
Motivation and summary:

text:
Motivation and summary:
This PR introduces a lightweight 'runtime-affinity prober' (affinity_prober) that runs short, deterministic probes across a small battery of inference shapes (seq lengths, batch sizes, kv-cache warm/cold) and collates an annotated performance map for model variants. The primary goal is to supply the model registry with structured, human-readable hints for release notes (e.g., 'suitable for 8k tokens with quantized-llm on A100; expected median p95 latency 42ms at batch=1') and to provide downstream deploy tooling with an initial affinity signal for placement and scheduling decisions.

What this changes (high level):
- registry/probes/affinity_prober.py: new probe runner with pluggable backends (mock backend for tests + runtime adapter).
- registry/annotators/perf_hint_exporter.py: turns probe output into compact annotations and suggested one-line changelog snipp...

title: policy-aware-onboard-reviewer-bot-and-compact-annotation-generator
preview: Context and motivation: Onboarding new model versions frequently requires cross-checking provenance, licenses, performance baselines, and safety policy assertions. Review cycles are slowed by repetitive checklist items and by non-standardized annotation formats in PRs. This change introduces a lightweight 'onboard-r...
text:
Context and motivation: Onboarding new model versions frequently requires cross-checking provenance, licenses, performance baselines, and safety policy assertions. Review cycles are slowed by repetitive checklist items and by non-standardized annotation formats in PRs. This change introduces a lightweight 'onboard-reviewer-bot' service and a compact annotation schema to accelerate reviews, surface policy-level risks in human-readable language, and produce suggested rollback manifests and changelog snippets when onboarding fails checks.

Primary goals: 1) Run deterministic policy checks (license, KMS usage, dataset license tags) and lightweight performance delta checks against stored baselines. 2) Present concise, human-readable annotations in the PR description so reviewers can make decisions quickly. 3) When a critical check fails, create a suggested rollback manifest (k8s manifest f...

title: Add usage-aware profile stitcher and autobrief generation for onboarding
preview: Motivation: When multiple intake profiles for the same model arrive from different partners/environments we need a deterministic, usage-weighted reconciliation so the registry surface (performance profiles, recommended quantization, changelog blurb) reflects real customer signal rather than noisy probes. This PR int...
text:
Motivation: When multiple intake profiles for the same model arrive from different partners/environments we need a deterministic, usage-weighted reconciliation so the registry surface (performance profiles, recommended quantization, changelog blurb) reflects real customer signal rather than noisy probes. This PR introduces a usage-aware 'profile stitcher' that merges incoming onboarding profiles using per-sample weights derived from telemetry and dataset provenance. It also adds an autobrief generator that drafts concise release notes summarizing changes between the active profile and the new stitched profile.

What this PR does (high level):
- Add profile stitcher implementation that: deduplicates metrics, normalizes latency/throughput histograms, applies per-field weighting, and resolves conflicting knobs (quantization/affordances) via configurable heuristics.
- Add autobrief genera...

title: Introduce probe-schema sampler and release-notes weaver
preview: Motivation: onboarding a model currently produces large, noisy changelogs and our sampling for profile calibration is expensive and sometimes biased by a few long sequences. This PR introduces (1) a small, explicit 'probe schema' for captured onboarding probes (tokenizer id, sequence length histogram, canonical prom...
text:
Motivation: onboarding a model currently produces large, noisy changelogs and our sampling for profile calibration is expensive and sometimes biased by a few long sequences. This PR introduces (1) a small, explicit 'probe schema' for captured onboarding probes (tokenizer id, sequence length histogram, canonical prompts, perf anchors) and (2) a sampler implementation that selects representative probe subsets by stratified length buckets + semantic clustering so downstream profiling and release-note diffs are stable and cheap. Additionally this PR adds a release-notes 'weaver' that synthesizes concise human-readable release blurbs from profile diffs and telemetry deltas (latency, token-cost, quality regression hints). The weaver outputs a compact markdown snippet and a machine-readable changelog fragment for the console UI.

What this PR changes (high level): added a probe schema and se...

title: Introduce sidecar metadata augmenter and contextual release clipper
preview: Motivation: Add a lightweight "sidecar" service that augments model profiles at intake time with runtime telemetry hints, provenance snippets and prioritized impact signals, and a companion 'release clipper' that generates minimal, contextual changelog fragments automatically for model version rollouts. This reduces...
text: Motivation: Add a lightweight "sidecar" service that augments model profiles at intake time with runtime telemetry hints, provenance snippets and prioritized impact signals, and a companion 'release clipper' that generates minimal, contextual changelog fragments automatically for model version rollouts. This reduces manual churn when onboarding models and helps signal which metadata deltas are likely to affect serving economics or quality. Design overview: (1) augmenter sidecar runs during profile ingestion and attaches: sampled latency histograms, hardware-affinity hints, embedding-dimension provenance, and a small 'culprit' tag computed by delta heuristics; (2) release clipper consumes profile metadata deltas and telemetry to emit a compact one-line changelog entry + 2-line rationale suitable for release notes and downstream consumers. Implementation highlights: new microservice wri...

title: Introduce annotated affinity stamps and chunked changelog compressor
preview: Motivation: onboarding high-capacity and quantized variants has become noisier in release notes and harder to map to hardware guidance for infra. This PR introduces two coordinated pieces: 1) "annotated affinity stamps" — a small, versioned metadata artifact attached to model variants that captures recommended hardw...
text:
Motivation: onboarding high-capacity and quantized variants has become noisier in release notes and harder to map to hardware guidance for infra. This PR introduces two coordinated pieces: 1) "annotated affinity stamps" — a small, versioned metadata artifact attached to model variants that captures recommended hardware affinity (e.g. ampere-80, triton-quant-fast, int8-friendly), expected memory working set, and recommended quantization knobs; and 2) a "chunked changelog compressor" — a post-processing step that consumes model intake diffs and emits condensed release chunks (human-friendly bullets + linked deep diffs) so changelogs stay readable for large model updates. Goals: reduce support noise during rollouts, surface deterministic hardware affinity suggestions to serving/runtime teams, and keep changelog summaries concise while preserving drill-down details.

title: Introduce staged intake intent and profile shadowing for safe, measurable model rollouts
preview: Context: As model intake velocity grows we need a lightweight safe path to stage new model manifests and performance profiles without impacting live serving. This PR adds a 'staged intake intent' concept plus 'profile shadowing' so teams can register candidate model versions, run sampled traffic/probes against shado...
text: Context: As model intake velocity grows we need a lightweight safe path to stage new model manifests and performance profiles without impacting live serving. This PR adds a 'staged intake intent' concept plus 'profile shadowing' so teams can register candidate model versions, run sampled traffic/probes against shadowed profiles, and automatically synthesize a preview changelog describing measured regressions and cost delta. Motivation: reduce risk of blind rollouts, make onboarding telemetry-driven, and provide concise release-ready notes for model ops. Summary of features: 1) staged intake manifest schema (staged_intake.yaml) with intent metadata (owner, canary-criteria, probe-sets, retention-ttl); 2) profile-shadow runner service that can accept a staged manifest and shadow live traffic into a sandboxed profile evaluation pipeline (local kv-cache isolation + cost telemetry); 3) prob...

title: Introduce intake sentinel and brief compiler for compact changelogs
preview: Context: onboarding churn and long autogenerated release notes make it hard for engineers to quickly assess risk and performance impact of new model variants. This PR adds two cooperating subsystems: an intake-sentinel that runs lightweight sanity and cost/perf heuristics during model intake, and a brief-compiler th...
text: Context: onboarding churn and long autogenerated release notes make it hard for engineers to quickly assess risk and performance impact of new model variants. This PR adds two cooperating subsystems: an intake-sentinel that runs lightweight sanity and cost/perf heuristics during model intake, and a brief-compiler that synthesizes compact, human-friendly changelog fragments and performance highlight cards. Motivation: reduce noise in release notes, accelerate review turnaround, and catch obvious regressions earlier in the pipeline. Scope: sentinel rules (schema checks, common-freeze tokens, latency/cost delta thresholds), telemetry-sampler hook (emits sampled perf traces for new variants), brief compiler (compact prose + delta table + example prompts), automation pipeline changes to attach compiled briefs to model uploads, and tests + migration. Checklist: - implement sentinel engine a...

title: Introduce lifecycle signatures and staged compatibility snippets for onboarding changelogs
preview: Background: onboarding churn and subtle compatibility regressions have made changelogs noisy and hard to consume. We need a small, machine-readable lifecycle signal attached to model profiles so release automation can produce concise, actionable changelog snippets when a model moves between lifecycle phases (onboard...
text:
Background: onboarding churn and subtle compatibility regressions have made changelogs noisy and hard to consume. We need a small, machine-readable lifecycle signal attached to model profiles so release automation can produce concise, actionable changelog snippets when a model moves between lifecycle phases (onboard -> staging -> prod). This PR introduces 'lifecycle_signature' metadata on model profiles and a staged compatibility-snippet generator that outputs human-friendly changelog fragments and suggested fallback mappings for deployments.

What this PR does: introduces a new lightweight lifecycle_signature schema (versioned), adds an onboarding hook to compute the signature, adds a changelog assembler plugin that emits staged compatibility snippets, and wires a CLI flag to preview generated snippets. The generator uses a deterministic fingerprint of (architecture, tokenizer_hash, ...

title: Introduce onboarding contract mapper and traffic-weighted release-notes generator
preview: Context / motivation: model onboarding churn has made release notes noisy: small metadata-only updates (typo fixes, curator comments) and large perf-affecting changes appear at the same verbosity level. Teams want concise, signal-rich changelogs that call out performance and runtime compatibility changes while suppr...
text:
Context / motivation: model onboarding churn has made release notes noisy: small metadata-only updates (typo fixes, curator comments) and large perf-affecting changes appear at the same verbosity level. Teams want concise, signal-rich changelogs that call out performance and runtime compatibility changes while suppressing low-impact edits. This PR adds two complementary pieces: (1) an onboarding contract mapper that normalizes and stamps the metadata that matters for serving/runtime compatibility, and (2) a traffic-weighted release-notes generator that scores deltas based on historical usage and staged rollout traffic to prioritize changelog sections.

What this PR does (high level):
- Introduce a lightweight onboarding_contract_mapper that: canonicalizes fields (model.aliases, quantization_profiles, runtime_hints), emits a stability fingerprint, and records a \"compatibility_contract...

title: Introduce embargoed provenance ledger and microdigest generator
preview: Context: customers and internal curation teams have asked for a way to attach time-bound/embargoed metadata to model intake that is cryptographically auditable and gated for release notes. This PR adds a small provenance ledger that signs embargoed metadata, exposes an ACL-aware retrieval path, and generates a compa...
text: Context: customers and internal curation teams have asked for a way to attach time-bound/embargoed metadata to model intake that is cryptographically auditable and gated for release notes. This PR adds a small provenance ledger that signs embargoed metadata, exposes an ACL-aware retrieval path, and generates a compact "microdigest" used by the Console and release automation to show concise delta-first release snippets. Motivation: reduce manual release-note drafting, centralize embargo handling for sensitive commercial or regulatory annotations, and produce deterministic microdigests for downstream QA and changelog automation. Changes: 1) API: intake endpoint accepts optional 'embargo_until' and 'embargo_reason' metadata; embargoed fields are stored encrypted and recorded in the provenance ledger with signer metadata. 2) Ledger: lightweight append-only ledger table with signer_id, sig...

title: Introduce variant affordance annotator and delta-aware changelog composer
preview: Context and motivation: We onboarded multiple model variants for the Q4 rollout and noticed reviewers and customers struggled to interpret subtle capability regressions across variants. Manual release notes were noisy and missed guidance for routing and mitigation. This PR introduces two complementary pieces: (1) a ...
text:
Context and motivation: We onboarded multiple model variants for the Q4 rollout and noticed reviewers and customers struggled to interpret subtle capability regressions across variants. Manual release notes were noisy and missed guidance for routing and mitigation. This PR introduces two complementary pieces: (1) a Variant Affordance Annotator that infers and stamps compact capability/constraint hints into variant metadata during intake, and (2) a Delta-aware Changelog Composer that synthesizes concise release notes by scoring profile deltas and surface-sampling representative examples.

What this delivers: - Affordance annotator: a modular analyzer that runs as part of the intake pipeline. It emits standardized tags such as "safety:high-sensitivity", "affordance:table-reading", "latency:snappy", "kvcache-friendly". Tags are stored on variant metadata and are consumed by routing heuri...

title: Add cohort-aware changelog enricher and perf baseline seeder
preview: Introduce a cohort-aware changelog enrichment pipeline and a perf baseline seeder to improve release notes quality and initial performance expectations for newly onboarded model variants. The change adds an enrichment worker that consumes sampled telemetry and onboarding probe results, computes sample-weighted delta...
text: Introduce a cohort-aware changelog enrichment pipeline and a perf baseline seeder to improve release notes quality and initial performance expectations for newly onboarded model variants. The change adds an enrichment worker that consumes sampled telemetry and onboarding probe results, computes sample-weighted deltas per cohort, and emits human-readable summary assertions and suggested baseline numbers for the release composer. A lightweight SQLite indexer records cohort sample weights and fast lookup keys to avoid recomputing on every run. The PR also adds a small CLI entrypoint (rmr changelog enrich) and a seed script that writes initial baseline entries into the model registry so downstream automation and dashboards have an initial reference. Motivation: reduce manual work for release authors, provide reproducible baseline numbers for canary rollouts, and improve changelog context ...

title: Compact probe serializer and autotag rationalizer for churn-resistant changelogs
preview: Context and motivation: The model onboarding pipeline currently emits verbose probe payloads and naive changelog diffs which cause noisy release notes and expensive downstream storage/transfer. This PR introduces a compact probe serializer (binary + content-addressed prefixes) and an autotag rationalizer that determ...
text:
Context and motivation: The model onboarding pipeline currently emits verbose probe payloads and naive changelog diffs which cause noisy release notes and expensive downstream storage/transfer. This PR introduces a compact probe serializer (binary + content-addressed prefixes) and an autotag rationalizer that deterministically picks minimal, stable tags for model profiles. The goal is to reduce changelog noise, make release diffs smaller, and improve the stability of model identifiers across quick re-uploads.

What this changes (high level): 1) CompactProbeSerializer: a new module that implements a size-aware serializer for probe samples. It uses a small binary encoding (protobuf-lite) combined with reservoir sampling for long prompts and optional bloom-filtered feature sketches for heavy fields so repeated telemetry is deduped early. 2) Fingerprint suffixes: content-addressed 8-byte ...

title: Consolidate onboard commit-capsule and auto-synthesize release briefs
preview: Motivation: onboarding currently scatters commit-level metadata, perf deltas, and release note fragments across artifacts and the model profile DB. That makes lightweight release summaries hard to generate and forces reviewers to cross-reference multiple sources. This change introduces a compact 'commit-capsule' blo...
text:
Motivation: onboarding currently scatters commit-level metadata, perf deltas, and release note fragments across artifacts and the model profile DB. That makes lightweight release summaries hard to generate and forces reviewers to cross-reference multiple sources. This change introduces a compact 'commit-capsule' blob that travels with a model profile (stored as signed metadata alongside the profile artifact) and a small pipeline to auto-synthesize concise release briefs from the capsule + perf deltas.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a commit-capsule format (minimal JSON) that contains: commit SHA, onboarding CLI user, dataset snapshot tag, top-level performance tokens (latency/throughput/embedding-l2), a short human author blurb, and a list of significant calibration flags (quantization/seq-len/kv-cache-behavior). 2) Extends the onboard CLI to emit the capsule and attach it to the profile...

title: Orchestrate onboarding DAG and release assembler for synthesized perf profiles
preview: Motivation: onboarding several new open models exposed a gap in how we compose metadata transforms, perf fragments, and release notes into a single deterministic model version artifact. This PR introduces an onboarding DAG (graph runner) that composes validators, normalizers, profile synthesizers, and a release-asse...
text: Motivation: onboarding several new open models exposed a gap in how we compose metadata transforms, perf fragments, and release notes into a single deterministic model version artifact. This PR introduces an onboarding DAG (graph runner) that composes validators, normalizers, profile synthesizers, and a release-assembler that produces canonical changelog/release-note entries for each model version. What changed: - new onboarding/graph package (DAG runner, node interfaces, light scheduler) - deterministic version hash: canonical serializer + version salt derived from provenance - perf profile synthesizer: merges infra benchmark fragments, manual annotations, and quantization notes into a single profile document with provenance traces - release-assembler: template-driven changelog entries + optional GitHub release draft action - CLI hooks and GitHub Action integration (onboard:run, onbo...

title: chronicle-writer: compact onboarding abstracts and deterministic release zipper
preview: Context: onboarding churn and release-note noise slow cross-team reviews. Large profile uploads include verbose provenance and full sample dumps; downstream consumers only need a small, deterministic artifact to verify compatibility and pull release summaries. This PR introduces the 'chronicle-writer' pipeline which...
text: Context: onboarding churn and release-note noise slow cross-team reviews. Large profile uploads include verbose provenance and full sample dumps; downstream consumers only need a small, deterministic artifact to verify compatibility and pull release summaries. This PR introduces the 'chronicle-writer' pipeline which emits a compact, deterministic zip (the 'chronicle') containing: a lightweight onboarding abstract (machine + human friendly), a sampled provenance manifest (configurable seed & reservoir size), delta fingerprints for fast diffs, and a concise release index used by downstream syncers. Motivation: speed up reviews, reduce artifact size for audits, and enable deterministic release replays for regressions. What this PR changes: 1) chronicle service: new CLI and server handler to build deterministic zips from profile uploads; 2) compact-onboard-abstract: a templated, schema-ba...

title: Affinity-guided changelog composer and manifest affinity stamps
preview: Motivation: make variant rollouts and capacity planning faster by surfacing runtime affinity signals on model manifests and by auto-drafting concise, priority-sorted changelog blurbs. This PR adds an opt-in 'affinity_stamp' metadata to manifests (hardware_family, os_kernel_tag, expected_latency_bucket), a small comp...
text: Motivation: make variant rollouts and capacity planning faster by surfacing runtime affinity signals on model manifests and by auto-drafting concise, priority-sorted changelog blurbs. This PR adds an opt-in 'affinity_stamp' metadata to manifests (hardware_family, os_kernel_tag, expected_latency_bucket), a small composer service that merges onboarding diffs with occupancy and sample-sufficiency heuristics to produce release snippets, and a migration/backfill tool that infers affinity from recent perf snapshots. The composer emits short human-readable blurbs and structured 'rollout_advisory' labels that downstream SRE and release automation can consume. Backwards compatibility: manifests without affinity_stamp are accepted and treated as 'affinity: unknown'. Feature-flagged rollout with dry-run and canary namespace. Linear links: ENG-10111 and ENG-9142. Related to automated release note...
